channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The 10 best songs competing at (a very contentious) Eurovision
item.description: Glitter, vocal gymnastics, on-stage flames — the show goes on on the Eurovision stage in Vienna, even though five countries are boycotting this year's contest due to Israel's participation.
item.content:encoded:
Glitter, vocal gymnastics, on-stage flames — the show goes on on the Eurovision stage in Vienna, even though five countries are boycotting this year's contest due to Israel's participation.

(Image credit: Tobias Schwarz)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: Native kids with disabilities were held in wooden boxes. Sweeping reforms are coming
item.description: State officials in New York say the Salmon River district's special education program confined young children with disabilities in wooden boxes. Parents weren't notified.
item.content:encoded:
State officials in New York say the Salmon River district's special education program confined young children with disabilities in wooden boxes. Parents weren't notified.

(Image credit: Used with permision)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Some plants have unusual genetics, which can help them weather cataclysmic events
item.description: Unlike humans, many plants have more than two sets of chromosomes. This trait may help them adapt to environmental upheaval, such as climate change.
item.content:encoded: Unlike humans, many plants have more than two sets of chromosomes. This trait may help them adapt to environmental upheaval, such as climate change.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: How AI is speeding new business creation, especially among Gen Z entrepreneurs
item.description: Entrepreneurs are turning to AI to speed the creation of new businesses, with Gen Z leading the way. That's according to a new report from the payroll software firm Gusto.
item.content:encoded: Entrepreneurs are turning to AI to speed the creation of new businesses, with Gen Z leading the way. That's according to a new report from the payroll software firm Gusto.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: The Supreme Court keeps abortion pill mifepristone available by telehealth
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled on Thursday that the abortion pill mifepristone can continue to be prescribed online or over the phone and sent through the mail.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court ruled on Thursday that the abortion pill mifepristone can continue to be prescribed online or over the phone and sent through the mail.

(Image credit: Jose Luis Magana)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Linda McMahon defends dismantling the Education Department, shifting its work
item.description: The education secretary faced questions about the shrinking of her agency, limits on federal student loan borrowing and oversight of the education of students with disabilities.
item.content:encoded:
The education secretary faced questions about the shrinking of her agency, limits on federal student loan borrowing and oversight of the education of students with disabilities.

(Image credit: Eric Lee)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Cuba's power grid collapses and plunges eastern provinces into a major blackout
item.description: Cuba's aging power grid has eroded in recent years as it faces a prolonged economic crisis, made worse by a U.S. energy blockade of the island.
item.content:encoded:
Cuba's aging power grid has eroded in recent years as it faces a prolonged economic crisis, made worse by a U.S. energy blockade of the island.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Jury orders Boeing to pay $49.5 million to family of 737 MAX crash victim
item.description: A federal jury in Chicago awarded $49.5 million to the family of Samya Stumo, a young woman who was killed in the second of two Boeing 737 MAX crashes within months of each other in 2018 and 2019.
item.content:encoded:
A federal jury in Chicago awarded $49.5 million to the family of Samya Stumo, a young woman who was killed in the second of two Boeing 737 MAX crashes within months of each other in 2018 and 2019.

(Image credit: Stumo Family)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: As Trump meets with Xi, security expert says China now faces the U.S. as a peer
item.description: Former national security official Rush Doshi says President Trump's sky-high tariffs on Chinese goods sparked a clash in which China prevailed. We look at the current state of U.S.-China relations.
item.content:encoded: Former national security official Rush Doshi says President Trump's sky-high tariffs on Chinese goods sparked a clash in which China prevailed. We look at the current state of U.S.-China relations.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: Researchers unearth Southeast Asia's largest dinosaur
item.description: They're calling it the "last titan" of Thailand. The sauropod — an herbivore with a long neck and tail — comes from the late Early Cretaceous period, some 100 to 120 million years ago.
item.content:encoded:
They're calling it the "last titan" of Thailand. The sauropod — an herbivore with a long neck and tail — comes from the late Early Cretaceous period, some 100 to 120 million years ago.

(Image credit: Patchanop Boonsai)
item.dc:creator: James Doubek
